verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is secreted, but its latent pro-form (LAP-TGFβ1 complex) is covalently disulfide-tethered to the transmembrane partners GARP (LRRC32) on Tregs/platelets and LRRC33 (NRROS) on myeloid cells, presenting latent TGFβ1 on the outer leaflet. This surface-tethered latent complex is a validated immuno-oncology antigen (e.g., GARP:TGFβ1 antibodies like livmoniplimab), satisfying covalent post-translational anchoring to a TM scaffold rather than mere transient recruitment.
